summary: Tests pass (115/115) but TypeScript compilation fails with 22 errors. The core issue: threadId→channelId rename was applied inconsistently — variable declarations were renamed but some interface fields, function parameters, and usage sites were missed. This causes both compile-time TS errors and runtime ReferenceErrors (undefined `threadId`/`channelId` variables). The new channel-service.ts is well-implemented. The bulk of the rename across API routes, state manager, types, and DB layers is correct.

issues.description: chat() public method options type still has `threadId?: string` but callers (handlers.ts:470) pass `channelId`. Rename to `channelId` to match executeChat()'s options type at line 111.
PendingEntry interface has `threadId: string` but findWaitingCustomText() at line 115 reads `entry.channelId`. Rename interface field to `channelId` and update register() at line 48.

ActiveProcess interface has `threadId?: string` but line 793 reads `active.channelId`. Rename field to `channelId`. Also fix line 163 which sets `threadId: options.channelId` — should be `channelId: options.channelId`.

sendChatInternal passes `channelId` to chat() options, but chat()'s type at client.ts:54 expects `threadId`. This property is silently ignored at runtime — Discord context system prompt won't be set. Fix by renaming chat()'s option type field (see client.ts issue).

Runtime bug: `mq.delete(threadId, msgId)` — `threadId` is not declared in this scope (was renamed to `channelId` at line 199). Progress messages won't be cleaned up after normal completion. Should be `mq.delete(channelId, msgId)`.
Runtime ReferenceError: `this.messageQueue.sendLong(threadId, ...)` — variable `threadId` was renamed to `channelId` at line 512 in this PR. Error message won't be sent. Should use `channelId`.
Runtime ReferenceError: `this.messageQueue.sendLong(threadId, ...)` — same issue as line 548, in idea qdev error handler. Should use `channelId`.
Runtime ReferenceError: `this.messageQueue.sendLong(threadId, ...)` — same issue as line 548, in idea-to-goal error handler. Should use `channelId`.

TS2339: ChannelUpdate/ChannelDelete event handlers access `newChannel.guild` / `channel.guild` but the Discord.js type union includes DMChannel which lacks `guild`. Add type guard before accessing `.guild`.
TS2769: `this.client.guilds.fetch(guildId)` — guildId is `string | undefined` (from config.authorizedGuildId) but fetch() requires `string`. Add guard: `if (!guildId) return;`
TS2554: startRefixPipeline calls runAudit() with 6 args but runAudit() requires 7 (missing `usage: ChatUsageResult`). Create an empty usage object and pass it.
TS2554: startRefixPipeline calls auditFixLoop() with 8 args but signature requires 9 (missing `usage: ChatUsageResult`). Pass the usage object.
executeTaskInBackground: parameter named `threadId` (line 1050) but body uses undeclared `channelId` at line 1057. Rename parameter to `channelId`.
runAudit: parameter named `threadId` (line 1396) but body uses undeclared `channelId` at lines 1403, 1413. Rename parameter to `channelId`.
TS2322: makeState() helper missing required `goalSeq` property. GoalDriveState.goalSeq is `number` (non-optional). Add `goalSeq: 1` to default.
syncFromDiscord uses hardcoded fallback cwd '/default' for new channels. Consider using guild default working directory instead.
errorReporter callback type still uses `threadId` parameter name. Cosmetic only (no runtime impact) but inconsistent with the rename convention.
